concerns: Step 5: Expanding tsconfig.json include to cover tests/ and *.config.ts will cause typecheck to fail on existing US-001 tests that use `__dirname` (a CJS global not available in ESNext/bundler module mode). Either add a separate tsconfig for tests, or keep the main tsconfig include as-is and let vitest handle its own TS compilation.
Step 6: The sample unit test should exercise a real pure function from src/ (even a trivial one) rather than an entirely self-contained test, to demonstrate the import/test pipeline works end-to-end.

Risk omission: The existing US-001 tests use `__dirname` which requires vitest to be configured with a Node-compatible environment and appropriate TS settings — the plan should note this compatibility concern.
Step 3: Consider also adding a `lint:fix` convenience script (`eslint src/ --fix`) since it's near-zero cost and commonly expected alongside a lint script.
